description_long: This obelisk is a dark, monolithic structure with a square base that tapers upward into a central column. The upper section is encircled by a crown of evenly spaced, upward-pointing, blade-like projections, giving it a fortified, almost ceremonial appearance. A decorative band with a repeating spiral motif wraps around the upper portion of the main shaft, just below the crown. One face of the obelisk features a geometric engraving, possibly symbolic or ornamental, while the other sides remain plain. The surface appears to be made of a dense, matte stone, lending the object a sense of permanence and gravitas. The base is broad and flat, providing stability and a platform-like presence suitable for urban plazas or ceremonial spaces.
description: A dark stone obelisk with a square base, spiral motif band, and a crown of blade-like projections at the top. One face features a geometric engraving, while the rest are plain and matte.
description_view_0: Front view showing the crown of projections, spiral motif band, and a plain face.
description_view_1: Rear view similar to the front, but with no visible engravings or markings.
description_view_2: Side view displaying the geometric engraving on the shaft, along with the crown and spiral motif.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, plain with no engravings, showing the crown and spiral motif.
attribute_inscription: One face features a geometric engraving resembling a stylized square or maze; no text or language is visible.
attribute_ornamentation: A decorative spiral motif band encircles the upper shaft, and the crown consists of blade-like projections; one face has a geometric engraving.
attribute_base_design: The base is a broad, square platform with a slightly raised plinth, constructed from the same dark stone as the shaft, and is undecorated except for its clean, angular lines.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is not a single point but a ring of upward-pointing, blade-like projections forming a crown, each with a sharp, angular finish.
